subject: [PATCH 2/8] mm: use zone lock guard in unset_migratetype_isolate()

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: No substantive comments have been made by other reviewers in this thread so far.

2026-03-06.patch_summary: This patch series aims to simplify the page isolation code in the Linux kernel by introducing a zone lock guard in unset_migratetype_isolate(). The current implementation uses an explicit lock/unlock pattern with a goto statement, which is replaced with automatic scope-based cleanup. This change improves code readability and reduces the risk of bugs.

2026-03-12.patch_summary: The patch replaces the explicit lock/unlock and goto pattern in unset_migratetype_isolate() with a zone lock guard, simplifying the code and reducing the risk of errors.

reviews.author: Dmitry Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: The patch author introduced a new approach to zone lock handling using the guard() function, replacing explicit lock/unlock patterns with automatic cleanup. The change is part of a larger series aimed at simplifying the mm code.

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch series aims to simplify the zone lock handling in the mm code by introducing a scope-based cleanup mechanism using the guard() function. The first patch, [PATCH 2/8], specifically targets the unset_migratetype_isolate() function and replaces the explicit lock/unlock pattern with automatic cleanup.
